changes.old: “Judges and sentences according to how he coils”
changes.new: “Judges and consigns according to how he coils”
changes.reason: Dante Inf. V.6: 'manda' = sends/dispatches/consigns (Minos consigning each soul to its circle), a verb distinct from 'giudica'; 'sentences' collapsed the two. 'Consigns' restores the dispatching sense (cf. Longfellow/Singleton 'sends', Hollander 'dispatches'). The originating finding of the drift audit, refuted by the verifier as borderline but confirmed against the Dante canon.
changes.context_sentence: Like a Minos who “Judges and consigns according to how he coils” he might have
